name: Manchuela and Alarcón Gorge Loop
name_original: Alarcón - El Picazo – circular por el río Júcar y el sendero de las Hoces de Alarcón
description: This difficult 30.8-mile mountain bike route circles the Júcar River and the Gorges of Alarcón, offering challenging terrain and historic vi
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alarcón offers diverse terrain shaped by the Júcar River, which carves deep ravines and gorges, most notably the "Hoz de Alarcón." The region features a dramatic landscape with a promontory where Alarcón Castle stands, surrounded by the river's closed bend. This unique geography provides varied topography, including riverbanks, historical bridges, and elevation changes, creating an engaging environment for mountain bike trails. The Alarcón Reservoir also contributes to the scenic backdrop for outdoor exploration. Best mountain bike trails around Alarcón The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse de Alarcón – circular desde Motilla del Palancar, a 34.8 miles (56 km) trail that takes 4 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Alarcón Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sendas y trialeras del Júcar – circular desde Quintanar del Rey por Villalgordo del Júcar y Casasimarro, a moderate 37.7 miles (61 km) path. This route follows the Júcar River, providing varied terrain and scenic river views. Local mountain bikers also love the Manchuela and Alarcón Gorge Loop, a 30.8 miles (50 km) trail leading through the dramatic Alarcón Gorge, often completed in about 4 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Alarcón is defined by the deep gorges of the Júcar River, varied topography, and historical landmarks like Alarcón Castle. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate routes along riverbanks to more challenging trails with significant elevation changes. The routes in Alarcón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alarcón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Alarcón?
A: There are nearly 40 mountain bike trails around Alarcón, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region's unique geography, shaped by the Júcar River, provides diverse terrain.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Alarcón?
A: Most mountain bike trails in Alarcón are rated as moderate, with 32 out of 38 routes falling into this category. There are also a few easy and difficult options, with 3 trails each, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Alarcón?
A: The terrain around Alarcón is highly varied, characterized by the deep gorges of the Júcar River, riverbanks, and significant elevation changes. You'll encounter paths along the river, through dramatic landscapes like the "Hoz de Alarcón," and near historical structures. Routes often feature a mix of natural paths and some excavated sections in limestone rock.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Alarcón?
A: Mountain biking in Alarcón offers stunning views of the Júcar River and its dramatic gorges, particularly the "Hoz de Alarcón." You can also see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Castle&lt;/a&gt; perched on a promontory, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5611845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henchidero Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and the serene Alarcón Reservoir. Highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail through the Gorges of Alarcón&lt;/a&gt; offer unique perspectives.

Q: Are there many circular mountain bike routes in Alarcón?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Alarcón are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017729121/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-desde-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón – circular desde Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017676470/sendas-y-trialeras-del-jucar-circular-desde-quintanar-del-rey-por-villalgordo-del-jucar-y-casasimarro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendas y trialeras del Júcar – circular desde Quintanar del Rey por Villalgordo del Júcar y Casasimarro&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Júcar River.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Alarcón?
A: While most trails are moderate, there are a few easier routes suitable for families or beginners. These trails typically have less demanding elevation changes and smoother paths, often found along the riverbanks or flatter sections away from the deepest gorges. Always check the route details for specific difficulty ratings.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Alarcón?
A: Many outdoor trails in natural areas like Alarcón are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations or specific trail signage, particularly in protected areas or near wildlife habitats. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the ride and carry water for them.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Alarcón?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Alarcón is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for more comfortable riding and better trail conditions.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alarcón?
A: The mountain bike trails in Alarcón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 3 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the dramatic scenery of the Júcar River gorges, and the historical landmarks like Alarcón Castle that provide a unique backdrop to their rides.

Q: Are there options for longer mountain bike tours in Alarcón?
A: Yes, Alarcón offers several longer mountain bike tours. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017684194/las-torcas-de-sisante-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Torcas de Sisante Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers over 55 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pimpollada Trail – Canal Diver Trail loop from Casas de Benítez&lt;/a&gt; is nearly 47 km. These routes provide extended riding experiences through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Alarcón?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not detailed, Alarcón, being a historic town and a popular destination, typically has designated parking areas. Look for public parking in or near the town center, or at the starting points of more popular routes. It's advisable to check maps or local information for the most convenient spots near your chosen trail.

Q: Are there accommodation options suitable for mountain bikers in Alarcón?
A: Alarcón offers various accommodation options, including the historic Alarcón Castle which now serves as a Parador. There are also smaller hotels, guesthouses, and rural accommodations in the surrounding area that cater to visitors. Many of these are well-suited for outdoor enthusiasts, providing a comfortable base after a day on the trails.
tips: An agricultural road in perfect condition that will allow you to give it a go and pick up a bit of speed through a typical landscape of the interior of Cuenca.
Nice and quiet place, ideal for taking a break next to the Júcar river, a few meters from the town center of El Picazo.

A very picturesque circular itinerary that runs partly through the Hoz del Río Júcar as it passes through the town of Alarcón. Beautiful views along the entire route, with areas with a lot of shade that make it ideal for the hot months.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Alarcón - El Picazo mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. It requires good physical condition and prior mountain biking experience. You should be prepared for sections where you might need to dismount and push your bike due to challenging terrain.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 49.6 kilometers long and typically takes around 4 hours and 5 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding, traversing the rugged Trail through the Gorges of Alarcón. You'll encounter narrow passages carved by the Júcar River into limestone rock, implying rocky, uneven, and potentially technical surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy magnificent views of the historic town of Alarcón, including its medieval walls and castle. The route also features the dramatic Gorges of Alarcón carved by the Júcar River, offering spectacular natural scenery. You'll also pass by the Casa de la Pradera and sections of the Vereda de los Serranos from Cuenca to Andalusia.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Alarcón - El Picazo route is a circular trail, meaning you will finish at your starting point.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: As Alarcón serves as both a starting and finishing point for this circular route, you can typically find parking within or near the historic town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Alarcón - El Picazo mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific restrictions for dogs on this particular route are not detailed, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas to protect wildlife and ensure their safety on challenging terrain. Always carry water for your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for mountain biking in this region. The weather is milder, and the natural vegetation, including pines, junipers, and poplars, is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike route. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths, including the Vuelta a la Manchuela, the Serndero de la Hoz de Alarcón, and the Vereda de los Serranos from Cuenca to Andalusia.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the Júcar River gorges?
faqs.answer: The diverse ecosystems of the Júcar River canyon and surrounding areas offer chances to observe various bird species and local mammals. The natural vegetation includes pines, junipers, poplars, ivy, and reeds.
